# solution.py
"""Team Collaboration Manager.

A thread-safe, in-memory collaboration platform that manages users, projects,
tasks, messaging (with attachments and threaded replies), a performance
dashboard (completion metrics and peer ratings) and reporting (project
progress, team performance and individual performance) with CSV and PDF
export.

Standard library only, ASCII only.

All mutating and reading operations are guarded by a single re-entrant lock
that is shared across every sub-store, so the manager is safe to use from
multiple threads. Objects returned to callers are deep copies, ensuring the
internal state cannot be mutated through a returned reference.
"""

import copy
import csv
import io
import threading
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum

__all__ = [
    "TeamCollaborationManager",
    "TaskStatus",
    "UserRole",
    "ReportFormat",
    "ReportType",
    "TeamCollaborationError",
    "ValidationError",
    "NotFoundError",
    "AuthorizationError",
    "DuplicateError",
    "ConflictError",
    "Attachment",
    "User",
    "Project",
    "Task",
    "Message",
    "Rating",
]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskStatus(Enum):
    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class UserRole(Enum):
    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error raised by the manager."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input fails validation."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an operation."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint is violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with current state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class Attachment(object):
    __slots__ = ("filename", "size", "uploaded_by", "uploaded_at")

    def __init__(self, filename, size, uploaded_by, uploaded_at):
        self.filename = filename
        self.size = size
        self.uploaded_by = uploaded_by
        self.uploaded_at = uploaded_at

    def __repr__(self):
        return "Attachment(filename={0!r}, size={1})".format(
            self.filename, self.size
        )


class User(object):
    __slots__ = ("id", "username", "email", "role", "created_at")

    def __init__(self, id, username, email, role, created_at):
        self.id = id
        self.username = username
        self.email = email
        self.role = role
        self.created_at = created_at

    def __repr__(self):
        return "User(id={0}, username={1!r})".format(self.id, self.username)


class Project(object):
    __slots__ = (
        "id",
        "name",
        "start_date",
        "end_date",
        "owner_id",
        "description",
    )

    def __init__(self, id, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description=""):
        self.id = id
        self.name = name
        self.start_date = start_date
        self.end_date = end_date
        self.owner_id = owner_id
        self.description = description

    def __repr__(self):
        return "Project(id={0}, name={1!r})".format(self.id, self.name)


class Task(object):
    __slots__ = (
        "id",
        "project_id",
        "title",
        "status",
        "assignee_id",
        "deadline",
        "started_at",
        "completed_at",
        "created_at",
    )

    def __init__(
        self,
        id,
        project_id,
        title,
        status,
        assignee_id,
        deadline,
        started_at,
        completed_at,
        created_at,
    ):
        self.id = id
        self.project_id = project_id
        self.title = title
        self.status = status
        self.assignee_id = assignee_id
        self.deadline = deadline
        self.started_at = started_at
        self.completed_at = completed_at
        self.created_at = created_at

    def __repr__(self):
        return "Task(id={0}, title={1!r}, status={2})".format(
            self.id, self.title, self.status
        )


class Message(object):
    __slots__ = (
        "id",
        "author_id",
        "body",
        "project_id",
        "task_id",
        "parent_id",
        "attachments",
        "created_at",
        "edited_at",
    )

    def __init__(
        self,
        id,
        author_id,
        body,
        project_id,
        task_id,
        parent_id,
        attachments,
        created_at,
        edited_at=None,
    ):
        self.id = id
        self.author_id = author_id
        self.body = body
        self.project_id = project_id
        self.task_id = task_id
        self.parent_id = parent_id
        self.attachments = attachments
        self.created_at = created_at
        self.edited_at = edited_at

    def __repr__(self):
        return "Message(id={0}, author_id={1})".format(self.id, self.author_id)


class Rating(object):
    __slots__ = (
        "id",
        "rater_id",
        "ratee_id",
        "score",
        "feedback",
        "task_id",
        "created_at",
    )

    def __init__(self, id, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback, task_id, created_at):
        self.id = id
        self.rater_id = rater_id
        self.ratee_id = ratee_id
        self.score = score
        self.feedback = feedback
        self.task_id = task_id
        self.created_at = created_at

    def __repr__(self):
        return "Rating(id={0}, score={1})".format(self.id, self.score)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Internal store
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _Store(object):
    """A lightweight keyed collection that shares the manager's lock.

    The shared ``_lock`` is what allows the manager to coordinate
    cross-store operations atomically while still exposing each domain as a
    distinct namespace (``manager.users``, ``manager.tasks`` ...).
    """

    def __init__(self, lock):
        self._lock = lock
        self.items = {}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Manager
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager(object):
    """Coordinates users, projects, tasks, messaging, performance and reports."""

    def __init__(self, now=None):
        # A single re-entrant lock is shared by every sub-store so that
        # multi-store operations (e.g. creating a task that validates a
        # project and an assignee) remain atomic.
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._clock = now if now is not None else datetime.now

        # Domain stores (each exposes the shared lock for thread-safety).
        self.users = _Store(self._lock)
        self.projects = _Store(self._lock)
        self.tasks = _Store(self._lock)
        self.messages = _Store(self._lock)
        self.performance = _Store(self._lock)  # items: rating_id -> Rating
        self.reports = _Store(self._lock)      # reports are computed on demand

        # Globally-unique id sequence shared across all entity types.
        self._id_seq = 0

        # Secondary indexes.
        self._username_index = {}              # username -> user_id
        self._email_index = {}                 # email -> user_id
        self._tasks_by_project = {}            # project_id -> [task_id]
        self._tasks_by_assignee = {}           # user_id -> [task_id]
        self._messages_by_project = {}         # project_id -> [message_id]
        self._messages_by_task = {}            # task_id -> [message_id]
        self._ratings_by_ratee = {}            # user_id -> [rating_id]

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Helpers
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def _next_id(self):
        with self._lock:
            self._id_seq += 1
            return self._id_seq

    def _now(self):
        return self._clock()

    @staticmethod
    def _clone(obj):
        return copy.deepcopy(obj)

    # -- internal "require" helpers (assume the lock is already held) -----
    def _require_user(self, user_id):
        user = self.users.items.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user

    def _require_project(self, project_id):
        project = self.projects.items.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project

    def _require_task(self, task_id):
        task = self.tasks.items.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task

    def _require_message(self, message_id):
        message = self.messages.items.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message

    @staticmethod
    def _is_blank(value):
        return not isinstance(value, str) or value.strip() == ""

    @classmethod
    def _valid_email(cls, email):
        return isinstance(email, str) and email.strip() != "" and "@" in email

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # User management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def register_user(self, username, email, role=UserRole.MEMBER):
        with self._lock:
            if self._is_blank(username):
                raise ValidationError("username must be a non-empty string")
            if not self._valid_email(email):
                raise ValidationError("email must contain '@' and be non-empty")
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
            if username in self._username_index:
                raise DuplicateError("username already exists")
            if email in self._email_index:
                raise DuplicateError("email already exists")

            user_id = self._next_id()
            user = User(user_id, username, email, role, self._now())
            self.users.items[user_id] = user
            self._username_index[username] = user_id
            self._email_index[email] = user_id
            return self._clone(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            return self._clone(self._require_user(user_id))

    def list_users(self):
        with self._lock:
            return [
                self._clone(self.users.items[uid])
                for uid in sorted(self.users.items)
            ]

    def update_user(self, user_id, username=None, email=None, role=None):
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            if username is not None:
                if self._is_blank(username):
                    raise ValidationError("username must be a non-empty string")
                if username != user.username and username in self._username_index:
                    raise DuplicateError("username already exists")
            if email is not None:
                if not self._valid_email(email):
                    raise ValidationError("invalid email")
                if email != user.email and email in self._email_index:
                    raise DuplicateError("email already exists")
            if role is not None and not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")

            if username is not None and username != user.username:
                self._username_index.pop(user.username, None)
                user.username = username
                self._username_index[username] = user_id
            if email is not None and email != user.email:
                self._email_index.pop(user.email, None)
                user.email = email
                self._email_index[email] = user_id
            if role is not None:
                user.role = role

            return self._clone(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            for project in self.projects.items.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:
                    raise ConflictError("user owns a project")

            for task_id in self._tasks_by_assignee.get(user_id, []):
                task = self.tasks.items.get(task_id)
                if task is not None and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    raise ConflictError("user has an open task")

            self.users.items.pop(user_id, None)
            self._username_index.pop(user.username, None)
            self._email_index.pop(user.email, None)
            self._tasks_by_assignee.pop(user_id, None)
            self._ratings_by_ratee.pop(user_id, None)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Project management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def create_project(self, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description=""):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(owner_id)
            if self._is_blank(name):
                raise ValidationError("project name must be a non-empty string")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot be before start_date")

            project_id = self._next_id()
            project = Project(
                project_id, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description
            )
            self.projects.items[project_id] = project
            self._tasks_by_project.setdefault(project_id, [])
            self._messages_by_project.setdefault(project_id, [])
            return self._clone(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            return self._clone(self._require_project(project_id))

    def list_projects(self):
        with self._lock:
            return [
                self._clone(self.projects.items[pid])
                for pid in sorted(self.projects.items)
            ]

    def update_project(
        self, project_id, name=None, start_date=None, end_date=None, description=None
    ):
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)

            new_start = start_date if start_date is not None else project.start_date
            new_end = end_date if end_date is not None else project.end_date
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot be before start_date")
            if name is not None and self._is_blank(name):
                raise ValidationError("project name must be a non-empty string")

            if name is not None:
                project.name = name
            if start_date is not None:
                project.start_date = start_date
            if end_date is not None:
                project.end_date = end_date
            if description is not None:
                project.description = description

            return self._clone(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)

            task_ids = list(self._tasks_by_project.get(project_id, []))
            for task_id in task_ids:
                task = self.tasks.items.pop(task_id, None)
                if task is not None and task.assignee_id is not None:
                    self._remove_from_index(
                        self._tasks_by_assignee, task.assignee_id, task_id
                    )
                for message_id in list(self._messages_by_task.get(task_id, [])):
                    self.messages.items.pop(message_id, None)
                self._messages_by_task.pop(task_id, None)
            self._tasks_by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            for message_id in list(self._messages_by_project.get(project_id, [])):
                self.messages.items.pop(message_id, None)
            self._messages_by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            self.projects.items.pop(project_id, None)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Task management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    @staticmethod
    def _remove_from_index(index, key, value):
        bucket = index.get(key)
        if bucket is not None and value in bucket:
            bucket.remove(value)

    def create_task(self, project_id, title, assignee_id=None, deadline=None):
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._require_user(assignee_id)
            if self._is_blank(title):
                raise ValidationError("task title must be a non-empty string")
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline cannot be before project start")

            task_id = self._next_id()
            task = Task(
                task_id,
                project_id,
                title,
                TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                assignee_id,
                deadline,
                None,
                None,
                self._now(),
            )
            self.tasks.items[task_id] = task
            self._tasks_by_project.setdefault(project_id, []).append(task_id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._tasks_by_assignee.setdefault(assignee_id, []).append(task_id)
            return self._clone(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id):
        with self._lock:
            return self._clone(self._require_task(task_id))

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            task_ids = sorted(self._tasks_by_project.get(project_id, []))
            return [self._clone(self.tasks.items[tid]) for tid in task_ids]

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            task_ids = sorted(self._tasks_by_assignee.get(user_id, []))
            return [self._clone(self.tasks.items[tid]) for tid in task_ids]

    def assign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            if task.assignee_id is not None:
                self._remove_from_index(
                    self._tasks_by_assignee, task.assignee_id, task_id
                )
            task.assignee_id = assignee_id
            self._tasks_by_assignee.setdefault(assignee_id, []).append(task_id)
            return self._clone(task)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id, new_assignee_id):
        return self.assign_task(task_id, new_assignee_id)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if task.assignee_id is not None:
                self._remove_from_index(
                    self._tasks_by_assignee, task.assignee_id, task_id
                )
                task.assignee_id = None
            return self._clone(task)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id, deadline):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._require_project(task.project_id)
                if deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline cannot be before project start")
            task.deadline = deadline
            return self._clone(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id, status):
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            now = self._now()

            if status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now

            task.status = status
            return self._clone(task)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Messaging
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id,
        body,
        project_id=None,
        task_id=None,
        attachments=None,
        parent_id=None,
    ):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(author_id)

            if project_id is None and task_id is None:
                raise ValidationError("a message needs a project or task target")
            if project_id is not None and task_id is not None:
                raise ValidationError("a message cannot target both project and task")
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)

            attachment_objs = self._build_attachments(attachments, author_id)

            if self._is_blank(body) and not attachment_objs:
                raise ValidationError("message body or an attachment is required")

            if parent_id is not None:
                self._require_message(parent_id)

            message_id = self._next_id()
            message = Message(
                message_id,
                author_id,
                body,
                project_id,
                task_id,
                parent_id,
                attachment_objs,
                self._now(),
                None,
            )
            self.messages.items[message_id] = message
            if project_id is not None:
                self._messages_by_project.setdefault(project_id, []).append(message_id)
            else:
                self._messages_by_task.setdefault(task_id, []).append(message_id)
            return self._clone(message)

    def _build_attachments(self, attachments, uploader_id):
        result = []
        if not attachments:
            return result
        now = self._now()
        for item in attachments:
            filename, data = item
            if self._is_blank(filename):
                raise ValidationError("attachment filename must be non-empty")
            size = len(data) if data is not None else 0
            result.append(Attachment(filename, size, uploader_id, now))
        return result

    def edit_message(self, message_id, editor_id, new_body):
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may edit a message")
            if self._is_blank(new_body) and not message.attachments:
                raise ValidationError("message body or an attachment is required")
            message.body = new_body
            message.edited_at = self._now()
            return self._clone(message)

    def add_attachment(self, message_id, uploader_id, filename, data):
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != uploader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may add attachments")
            if self._is_blank(filename):
                raise ValidationError("attachment filename must be non-empty")
            size = len(data) if data is not None else 0
            message.attachments.append(
                Attachment(filename, size, uploader_id, self._now())
            )
            return self._clone(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            message_ids = sorted(self._messages_by_project.get(project_id, []))
            return [self._clone(self.messages.items[mid]) for mid in message_ids]

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_task(task_id)
            message_ids = sorted(self._messages_by_task.get(task_id, []))
            return [self._clone(self.messages.items[mid]) for mid in message_ids]

    def delete_message(self, message_id, requester_id):
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != requester_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may delete a message")
            self.messages.items.pop(message_id, None)
            if message.project_id is not None:
                self._remove_from_index(
                    self._messages_by_project, message.project_id, message_id
                )
            if message.task_id is not None:
                self._remove_from_index(
                    self._messages_by_task, message.task_id, message_id
                )

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Performance dashboard
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def _assigned_tasks(self, user_id):
        return [
            self.tasks.items[tid]
            for tid in self._tasks_by_assignee.get(user_id, [])
            if tid in self.tasks.items
        ]

    @staticmethod
    def _completion_seconds(task):
        if task.started_at is not None and task.completed_at is not None:
            return (task.completed_at - task.started_at).total_seconds()
        return None

    @classmethod
    def _completed_durations(cls, tasks):
        """Return the completion duration (seconds) of every completed task.

        Computed in a single pass so callers never evaluate the same
        timedelta twice.
        """
        durations = []
        for task in tasks:
            if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                seconds = cls._completion_seconds(task)
                if seconds is not None:
                    durations.append(seconds)
        return durations

    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._assigned_tasks(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            return completed / float(len(tasks))

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            durations = self._completed_durations(self._assigned_tasks(user_id))
            if not durations:
                return None
            return sum(durations) / float(len(durations))

    def submit_rating(self, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback="", task_id=None):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(rater_id)
            self._require_user(ratee_id)
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("a user cannot rate themselves")
            if not isinstance(score, int) or isinstance(score, bool):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer between 1 and 5")
            if score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be between 1 and 5")
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)

            rating_id = self._next_id()
            rating = Rating(
                rating_id, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback, task_id, self._now()
            )
            self.performance.items[rating_id] = rating
            self._ratings_by_ratee.setdefault(ratee_id, []).append(rating_id)
            return self._clone(rating)

    def _ratings_for(self, user_id):
        return [
            self.performance.items[rid]
            for rid in self._ratings_by_ratee.get(user_id, [])
            if rid in self.performance.items
        ]

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / float(count) if count else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": [self._clone(r) for r in ratings],
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._assigned_tasks(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            completion_rate = completed / float(total) if total else 0.0

            durations = self._completed_durations(tasks)
            avg_time = (
                sum(durations) / float(len(durations)) if durations else None
            )

            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / float(rating_count)
                if rating_count
                else 0.0
            )

            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": completion_rate,
                "average_completion_time": avg_time,
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Reports
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def _task_metrics(self, tasks):
        total = len(tasks)
        completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
        in_progress = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
        not_started = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED)
        completion_rate = completed / float(total) if total else 0.0
        durations = self._completed_durations(tasks)
        avg_time = sum(durations) / float(len(durations)) if durations else None
        return {
            "total_tasks": total,
            "completed_tasks": completed,
            "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
            "not_started_tasks": not_started,
            "completion_rate": completion_rate,
            "average_completion_time": avg_time,
        }

    def _rating_metrics(self, user_id):
        ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
        count = len(ratings)
        average = sum(r.score for r in ratings) / float(count) if count else 0.0
        return count, average

    def project_progress_report(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            task_ids = self._tasks_by_project.get(project_id, [])
            tasks = [self.tasks.items[tid] for tid in task_ids if tid in self.tasks.items]
            now = self._now()

            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            overdue = 0
            per_assignee = {}
            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
                if (
                    task.deadline is not None
                    and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                    and task.deadline < now
                ):
                    overdue += 1
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    per_assignee.setdefault(task.assignee_id, []).append(task)

            total = len(tasks)
            completed = status_counts[TaskStatus.COMPLETED.value]
            completion_rate = completed / float(total) if total else 0.0

            per_assignee_summary = {}
            for assignee_id, assignee_tasks in per_assignee.items():
                metrics = self._task_metrics(assignee_tasks)
                per_assignee_summary[assignee_id] = {
                    "total_tasks": metrics["total_tasks"],
                    "completed_tasks": metrics["completed_tasks"],
                    "completion_rate": metrics["completion_rate"],
                }

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": completion_rate,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee_summary,
                "generated_at": now,
            }

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id=None):
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
                task_ids = self._tasks_by_project.get(project_id, [])
                relevant_tasks = [
                    self.tasks.items[tid]
                    for tid in task_ids
                    if tid in self.tasks.items
                ]
            else:
                relevant_tasks = list(self.tasks.items.values())

            tasks_by_user = {}
            for task in relevant_tasks:
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    tasks_by_user.setdefault(task.assignee_id, []).append(task)

            rows = []
            for user_id in sorted(tasks_by_user):
                user = self.users.items.get(user_id)
                metrics = self._task_metrics(tasks_by_user[user_id])
                rating_count, rating_average = self._rating_metrics(user_id)
                row = {
                    "user_id": user_id,
                    "username": user.username if user is not None else None,
                    "total_tasks": metrics["total_tasks"],
                    "completed_tasks": metrics["completed_tasks"],
                    "completion_rate": metrics["completion_rate"],
                    "average_completion_time": metrics["average_completion_time"],
                    "rating_count": rating_count,
                    "rating_average": rating_average,
                }
                rows.append(row)

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
                "generated_at": self._now(),
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._assigned_tasks(user_id)
            metrics = self._task_metrics(tasks)
            rating_count, rating_average = self._rating_metrics(user_id)
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "username": user.username,
                "total_tasks": metrics["total_tasks"],
                "completed_tasks": metrics["completed_tasks"],
                "in_progress_tasks": metrics["in_progress_tasks"],
                "not_started_tasks": metrics["not_started_tasks"],
                "completion_rate": metrics["completion_rate"],
                "average_completion_time": metrics["average_completion_time"],
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
                "tasks": [self._clone(t) for t in sorted(tasks, key=lambda x: x.id)],
                "generated_at": self._now(),
            }

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Report export
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def export_report(self, report, report_format):
        if not isinstance(report, dict):
            raise ValidationError("report must be a dict")
        if not isinstance(report_format, ReportFormat):
            raise ValidationError("report_format must be a ReportFormat")
        if report_format == ReportFormat.CSV:
            return self._report_to_csv(report)
        return self._report_to_pdf(report)

    @staticmethod
    def _report_to_csv(report):
        output = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(output)
        report_type = report.get("report_type")

        if report_type == ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value:
            columns = [
                "user_id",
                "username",
                "total_tasks",
                "completed_tasks",
                "completion_rate",
                "average_completion_time",
                "rating_count",
                "rating_average",
            ]
            writer.writerow(columns)
            for row in report.get("rows", []):
                writer.writerow([row.get(col, "") for col in columns])

        elif report_type == ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value:
            columns = [
                "user_id",
                "username",
                "total_tasks",
                "completed_tasks",
                "in_progress_tasks",
                "not_started_tasks",
                "completion_rate",
                "average_completion_time",
                "rating_count",
                "rating_average",
            ]
            writer.writerow(columns)
            writer.writerow([report.get(col, "") for col in columns])

        else:  # project_progress or any other dict-shaped report
            writer.writerow(["metric", "value"])
            for key in (
                "project_id",
                "total_tasks",
                "completion_rate",
                "overdue_tasks",
            ):
                if key in report:
                    writer.writerow([key, report[key]])
            for status_value, count in report.get("status_counts", {}).items():
                writer.writerow(["status_" + str(status_value), count])

        return output.getvalue()

    @classmethod
    def _report_to_pdf(cls, report):
        lines = cls._report_text_lines(report)
        return cls._build_pdf(lines)

    @staticmethod
    def _report_text_lines(report):
        lines = []
        report_type = report.get("report_type", "report")
        lines.append("Report: {0}".format(report_type))
        for key, value in report.items():
            if key == "report_type":
                continue
            if key == "rows":
                lines.append("rows: {0}".format(len(value)))
            elif key == "tasks":
                lines.append("tasks: {0}".format(len(value)))
            elif key == "status_counts" and isinstance(value, dict):
                for status_value, count in value.items():
                    lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(status_value, count))
            elif isinstance(value, (str, int, float)):
                lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, value))
            elif value is None:
                lines.append("{0}: -".format(key))
        return lines

    @staticmethod
    def _build_pdf(text_lines):
        """Build a minimal but structurally valid single-page PDF document."""

        def escape(text):
            return (
                text.replace("\\", "\\\\")
                .replace("(", "\\(")
                .replace(")", "\\)")
            )

        content_parts = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "72 720 Td", "14 TL"]
        first = True
        if not text_lines:
            text_lines = [""]
        for line in text_lines:
            ascii_line = escape(line).encode("ascii", "replace").decode("ascii")
            if first:
                content_parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(ascii_line))
                first = False
            else:
                content_parts.append("T* ({0}) Tj".format(ascii_line))
        content_parts.append("ET")
        content = "\n".join(content_parts).encode("ascii")

        objects = [
            b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
            b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
            (
                b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
                b"/Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> /Contents 4 0 R >>"
            ),
            b"<< /Length "
            + str(len(content)).encode("ascii")
            + b" >>\nstream\n"
            + content
            + b"\nendstream",
            b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
        ]

        pdf = bytearray(b"%PDF-1.4\n")
        offsets = []
        for index, obj in enumerate(objects, start=1):
            offsets.append(len(pdf))
            pdf += str(index).encode("ascii") + b" 0 obj\n" + obj + b"\nendobj\n"

        xref_position = len(pdf)
        count = len(objects) + 1
        pdf += b"xref\n0 " + str(count).encode("ascii") + b"\n"
        pdf += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
        for offset in offsets:
            pdf += ("%010d 00000 n \n" % offset).encode("ascii")
        pdf += (
            b"trailer\n<< /Size "
            + str(count).encode("ascii")
            + b" /Root 1 0 R >>\n"
        )
        pdf += b"startxref\n" + str(xref_position).encode("ascii") + b"\n%%EOF"
        return bytes(pdf)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Tiny smoke demonstration; the authoritative behaviour is in the tests.
    manager = TeamCollaborationManager()
    alice = manager.register_user("alice", "alice@example.com", UserRole.ADMIN)
    project = manager.create_project(
        "Apollo", datetime.now(), datetime.now(), alice.id, "demo project"
    )
    task = manager.create_task(project.id, "Kickoff", assignee_id=alice.id)
    manager.update_status(task.id, TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
    report = manager.individual_performance_report(alice.id)
    print("Completion rate:", manager.get_completion_rate(alice.id))
    print("CSV export length:", len(manager.export_report(report, ReportFormat.CSV)))
    print("PDF export prefix:", manager.export_report(report, ReportFormat.PDF)[:8])
